meta.name: Cristian Hernandez
meta.title: Content Designer & AI Systems
meta.tagline: Designing clarity at the intersection of language and technology.

content.title: AI Enablement
content.subtitle: Content experience for IBMers
content.body:
## Overview
* Led content design and strategy for the AI Enablement initiative at IBM
    * AI Enablement: Help technical and non-technical IBMers learn and use internal generative AI solutions to increase productivity.
* Designed content for various AI topics (e.g., AI foundations, standards and compliance, developer guidance, watsonx, and governance)
* Increased integration of AI in IBM apps by 28% (+231 apps enabled in one month)

content.title: ibm-style-editor
content.subtitle: Automated IBM Style editorial pipeline for .docx and .pdf
content.body:
A workflow skill that orchestrates a full IBM Style editorial review on a `.docx` or `.pdf` document. It runs deterministic linting stages (Vale, sentence streamlining, grammar, UI compliance) followed by three sequential LLM passes that annotate the original document with Word balloon comments or PDF sticky notes — leaving a `_MasterReview` file the writer can work from directly.

Designed to reduce the manual effort of style reviews while preserving the writer's voice. The pipeline is self-evaluating: it scores its own output after each run.

content.title: ibm-style-coach
content.subtitle: Writing coach for IBM Style compliance
content.body:
A coaching skill that helps writers improve their IBM Style compliance — without just handing them the corrections. Works across three modes: DOCX, PDF, and inline text pasted directly into chat.

For each violation found, the coach explains *why* the rule exists, what to watch for going forward, and offers a suggested rewrite on the first occurrence only — so writers learn the pattern, not just accept a fix. Designed around the principle that good editorial feedback builds writer capability over time.
